basics.name: Giorgi Dundua
basics.label: Software Engineer (Backend/DevOps)
summary: Software Engineer with hands-on project experience in Python backend development and DevOps tooling (Docker, GitHub Actions, Fly.io), and a professional background spanning software delivery, QA, and technical support. Chalmers Software Engineering alumnus targeting backend and infrastructure roles

skills.name: Languages
keywords: Swedish - Working proficiency in reading and written communication

projects.name: Personal Data Platform
highlights: Architected a stateless, idempotent DAG pipeline to ingest and normalize heterogeneous health telemetry (API & CSV)
Implemented Atomic I/O patterns (Write-Ahead Log) and Content-Addressable caching to ensure data integrity and eliminate redundant compute
Engineered portable execution state across local Docker and GitHub Actions environments using Google Drive as a shared persistent store
Deployed a dual-mode Streamlit dashboard via multi-stage Docker build and CI/CD pipeline, with secure authentication and automated mock data generation for public demos

projects.name: NordTech Corporate Platform
highlights: Containerized a production Flask application utilizing slim base images and cache-optimized layer caching, reducing image footprint by 85% for rapid deployment
Configured rolling deployments via Fly.io with GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for automated, zero-downtime releases
Refactored legacy codebase into the Application Factory Pattern, enforcing strict separation of configuration and secrets per 12-Factor App principles
Engineered asynchronous background threading for non-blocking email delivery, decoupling I/O from the request cycle

work.name: Independent
highlights: Engineered and deployed a production-grade corporate web platform in Python using Flask, multi-stage Docker builds, and Fly.io microVMs
Architected an idempotent DAG data pipeline for health telemetry utilizing SQLite, Pydantic, and GitHub Actions CI/CD
Building Kubernetes expertise through structured curriculum covering containerization, orchestration, and cloud-native patterns

work.name: NordTech Solutions
highlights: Worked with CTO and clients to define system scope, team composition, and technical estimates; broke requirements into structured task breakdowns for delivery teams
Oversaw QA across client platforms — applied Selenium automation selectively where test stability justified it; used manual testing for UI-heavy and context-dependent flows
Managed delivery risks, incidents, and client communications across full project lifecycles; coordinated commercial negotiations, and technical hiring (CV screening, interviews)
Travelin.ai: multi-module booking and cost reporting platform for corporate travel; led QA strategy, task distribution, and release coordination
Vavisjon.no: resource management monitoring platform for Norwegian municipalities; tasked Python/React/DevOps team, reviewed deliverables and managed delivery

work.name: TIBCO Software Inc.
highlights: Supported enterprise multi-tier Spotfire deployments: Java/Tomcat server, .NET Node Manager services, Oracle/MSSQL backends across Windows and Linux — including clustering, load balancing, and high-memory Web Player nodes

Diagnosed complex production issues through server log analysis, local environment reproduction, and direct collaboration with the Spotfire development team in Gothenburg — covering memory leaks, SSO/Kerberos, SSL/TLS, and infrastructure misconfigurations

Authored knowledge base articles from root-cause findings and delivered infrastructure-level workarounds for issues pending engineering fixes; on track for Senior SSE promotion prior to collective department elimination by US head office

work.name: German Butcher LTD
highlights: Worked as a junior backend developer on a custom internal system for production, inventory, budgeting, and reporting
Implemented backend features and business logic under guidance from senior developers, gaining practical experience with real‑world enterprise workflows
Assisted with data handling and basic reporting, improving visibility into production and cost structures

work.name: Gullinbursti LTD
highlights: Built and managed supplier relationships for food production inputs across the EU, focusing on cross‑border imports into Georgia
Conducted supplier research, outreach, and commercial negotiations, coordinating with freight forwarders and local manufacturers
Managed day‑to‑day operations related to deliveries, pricing, and basic financial follow‑up

education.institution: Chalmers University of Technology & University of Gothenburg
highlights: Joint degree with a strong focus on long‑running industrial projects covering the full software development lifecycle
Core areas: software architecture (distributed and embedded), software quality and testing, and process improvement
Developed strong skills at the intersection of technical engineering and change management in software organisations

education.institution: School of Business, Economics and Law, University of Gothenburg
highlights: Studied corporate finance, financial markets, and quantitative analysis, building complementary analytical and business skills
